LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GOOGLE, INC. AND ALPHABET, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED AND MS. MARTINEZ' PAGA CLAIMS SEEKING CIVIL PENALTIES ARE STAYED PENDING RESOLUTION OF THE ARBITRATION OF PLAINTIFF THERESA MARTINEZ'S INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE PREVAILING PARTY IN THIS MOTION SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, J. Jason Hill of the law offices of Cohelan Khoury & Singer (619-595-3001) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Katherine C. Huibonhoa of the law offices of Grube Brown & Geidt LLP (415-603-5008) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANTS GOOGLE, INC. and ALPHABET, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration is GRANTED and Ms. Martinez' PAGA claims seeking civil penalties are stayed pending resolution of the arbitration of plaintiff Theresa Martinez's individual claims. Google has shown that Ms. Martinez agreed to the Dispute Resolution Agreement and that Agreement is not an unenforceable browsewrap agreement. Ms. Martinez was prompted to take an affirmative action and was given clear and conspicuous notice that by taking it she agreed to the Agreement, which was hyperlinked and accompanied by a notice that she was waiving her rights to collective action. The Federal Arbitration Act governs the Agreement and the section 1 exemption does not apply. Ms. Martinez failed to meet her burden of demonstrating that she belonged to a class of transportation workers engaged in interstate commerce. There is no evidence that interstate deliveries were ever made nor has Ms. Martinez presented evidence demonstrating that, despite the express terms of the Agreement, an employment relationship existed between her and Google. Ms. Martinez's claims are within the scope of arbitration agreement with the exception of her PAGA claims that seek civil penalties and not recovery of unpaid wages. Although the Agreement has a degree of procedural unconscionability as a contract of adhesion, there is no substantive unconscionability and thus the Agreement is not unconscionable. The ruling is STAYED pending a decision by the Supreme Court in a related action. Rather than set a control date, the Court leaves it to the parties to return and seek further orders from this Court once the Supreme Court has ruled. The prevailing party in this motion shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No. 8098, e-mail: casey.giselle@gmail.com (L&M302)
